title: penducella monilia
intro: Penducella monilia, commonly known as Indian pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including the improvement of cognitive function, memory, and mood, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The therapeutic actions of Penducella monilia include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce anxiety and stress, and enhance the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Penducella monilia can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use of the plant's extracts can also cause liver and kidney damage, as well as interact with certain medications. Furthermore, improper handling and preparation of the plant can result in respiratory problems, such as asthma-like symptoms and shortness of breath.
